_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Kidney Stones (Post Open Pyelolithotomy Follow Up)
clinical_indications: Stone composition (ie, cystine), or anatomy (ie, ectopic, pelvic, or horseshoe kidney).

In cases of Obstruction of a caliceal infundibulum, the ureteropelvic junction, or the lumbar ureter and when volume and configuration of the stones contraindicate extracorporeal shock wave lithotripsy (ESWL) or percutaneous approach.
Such as with caliceal stones larger than the renal pelvis. minimally branched staghorn stones in the renal pelvis and excessive morbid obesity.
patients who are undergoing major open abdominal or retroperitoneal surgical procedures for other indications;
the most common concomitant procedure is open pyeloplasty for ureteropelvic junction (UPJ) obstruction.

Discharge summary of last pyelolithotomy Open. procedure performed

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes detailing signs and symptoms, previous surgery/procedure, follow-up visit details, advise for daycare procedure submitted?

Was the USG/Urine routine, drugs prescribed submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes detailing signs and symptoms, previous surgery/procedure, follow-up visit details, or advice for daycare procedure missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Discharge summary of the last open pyelolithotomy procedure not submitted at pre-authorization.
Detailed clinical notes of the current visit missing or incomplete at claim submission.
USG/Urine routine or prescribed drugs not submitted at claim submission.
Discharge summary of the last open pyelolithotomy procedure not indicative of a follow-up visit.
The current request is for a 'Follow Up' package, but the clinical documentation does not clearly support a follow-up visit after an Open Pyelolithotomy.

clinical_key_pointers: Pyelolithotomy: is procedure of choice for stones within the renal pelvis, including stones that demonstrated minimal invasion into calyces and infundibulum.
After introducing extracorporeal shockwave lithotripsy (ESWL) and percutaneous nephropyelolithotomy (PCN), pyelolithotomy has become an uncommon surgery in most countries
additional_information.post_op_care: An imaging study to confirm the removal of all stone particle, prescribing drugs and Patient education (as part of follow up).
